name: Seonji Kim
statement: Designing Spatial Experiences, Building Adaptive Spaces, and Researching Spatial Intelligence.
statementHtml: Designing Spatial Experiences, Building Adaptive Spaces, and Researching Spatial Intelligence.
about:
I am a Ph.D. student in the Graduate School of Culture Technology at KAIST and a member of the Ubiquitous Virtual Reality Lab, advised by Prof. Woontack Woo. My research aims to create adaptive spatial experiences mediated by physical environments, viewing space not merely as a backdrop but as an active medium for interaction, perception, and collaboration. My previous work has focused on establishing spatial correspondences across heterogeneous environments to support shared mixed-reality experiences. I am currently exploring AI-driven approaches that interpret collaborative contexts and physical environments to infer spatial requirements and configure adaptive MR workspaces.

More broadly, I aim to advance spatial computing toward intelligent systems that can perceive, reason about, and adapt to the physical world, with broader connections to embodied and physical AI. I am currently seeking postdoctoral opportunities to further pursue and expand this research direction.
